description_long: This house features a blocky, modular design with three distinct sections, each topped with a flat, gray roof. The facade is primarily a warm orange-brown brick with decorative brickwork accents at the corners and around the windows. The front section displays three stories with arched windows arranged in symmetrical rows, and a blue main door at street level. The side and rear sections are simpler, with fewer windows and minimal ornamentation, but maintain the same brick and roof style. The windows are outlined in white, and the doors are painted blue, adding contrast to the brick exterior. The overall appearance suggests a compact, urban residential building, possibly a townhouse or row house, suitable for dense city neighborhoods.
description: A three-section urban house with flat gray roofs, orange-brown brick walls, and symmetrical arched windows. Blue doors and white window frames add visual contrast to the blocky, modular structure.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main facade with three stories, arched windows in symmetrical rows, and a blue entry door.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the blue side door, fewer windows, and the stepped arrangement of the building sections.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a single arched window and the plain brickwork of the back sections, with minimal ornamentation.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the largest section with a blue door and arched windows, and the smallest section with plain brickwork.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a blue single door at street level, no porch, and a simple overhang.
